health data as special category requiring additional protection

redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism

results.label: DPA — well drafted, minimal issues expected
missingClauses: Record of processing activities assistance (GDPR Art. 30) — Processor obligation to maintain and share processing records
Data Protection Impact Assessment assistance (GDPR Art. 35) — Processor obligation to assist Controller with DPIAs
Data Protection Officer contact details (GDPR Art. 37-39) — where applicable
Annex I: Subject matter, nature, purpose, duration, data types, and categories of data subjects (GDPR Art. 28(3))
UK transfer mechanism (UK IDTA or UK Addendum) where UK data subjects are in scope
positiveClauses: Clause 2 — Scope and Instructions: Clean, mirrors GDPR Art. 28(3)(a) language precisely
Clause 3 — Confidentiality: Correctly imposes confidentiality on authorised persons per GDPR Art. 28(3)(b)
Clause 4 — Security (Article 32 GDPR): Specific and strong — AES-256, TLS 1.3, access controls, and regular testing are named explicitly
Clause 5 — Sub-processors: Prior written authorisation, named sub-processor list, and 30-day advance notice of changes — meets GDPR Art. 28(2)
Clause 6 — Data Subject Rights: Covers all GDPR Arts. 15-22 rights with a defined 5-business-day response window
Clause 7 — Breach Notification: 24-hour notification window is stricter than GDPR's 72-hour requirement — Controller-friendly and appropriate
Clause 9 — Deletion: 30-day deletion or return window with written certification — complete and well-drafted

results.label: DPA — HIPAA context, missing BAA obligations
missingClauses: Subcontractor / subprocessor obligations — BAA must require Business Associate to obtain HIPAA-compliant BAAs with any subcontractors who receive PHI (45 CFR §164.504(e)(2)(ii)(D))
Individual rights obligations — Business Associate must support Covered Entity in fulfilling access, amendment, and accounting of disclosures requests under 45 CFR §164.524–164.528
HHS inspection and audit rights — Business Associate must make internal practices and records available to HHS for compliance review (45 CFR §164.504(e)(2)(ii)(H))
Minimum necessary standard — Business Associate must limit PHI use and disclosure to the minimum necessary to accomplish the permitted purpose
Workforce training and sanctions — HIPAA Security Rule requires documented workforce training and a sanctions policy for workforce members who violate PHI policies
Contingency and disaster recovery plan — required under HIPAA Security Rule §164.308(a)(7) for a cloud-based system storing full EHR data
positiveClauses: Section 1 — Services: clearly identifies the service scope and PHI categories processed, which is a useful foundation

issues: Red flags: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards
